content.text: Integrating Claude into the tools an organisation already uses — Slack, Google Workspace, Microsoft 365, GitHub, Jira, and CRM systems — is where AI adoption becomes visible to employees who might never interact with claude.ai directly. Each tool has its own integration surface, and the right approach depends on whether engineering resources are available, whether the workflow is interactive or automated, and how much conversational context needs to persist.

attrs.alt: Claude integration patterns across Slack, Google Workspace, Microsoft 365, GitHub, Jira, and CRM systems

content.text: The integration map above covers the six most common enterprise tool categories. Cowork OAuth connectors handle the interactive human-in-the-loop cases for supported tools. MCP servers and the direct Anthropic API handle custom systems and automated pipelines.
Slack Integration

Slack is the highest-value integration target for most teams because it is where real-time communication happens and where employees already context-switch constantly. A well-implemented Claude bot in Slack reduces the friction of 'go to claude.ai, explain context, copy result back to Slack' to a single in-channel interaction.

attrs.alt: Three-tier Slack bot architecture from simple slash command to persistent multi-tool assistant

content.text: Tier 1: Slash Command Bot

The simplest integration. A user types `/ask-claude What is our refund policy for digital products?` and Claude's response appears in the channel. The implementation is a stateless HTTP endpoint that receives a Slack slash command payload and calls the Anthropic API.

The async pattern (respond immediately with 'Thinking...', then use the `response_url` to post the real answer) is required because Slack times out slash command responses after 3 seconds. Claude's API call takes 2–10 seconds depending on the model and response length.
Tier 2: @Mention Bot with Thread Context
The mention bot listens for `@ClaudeBot` in any channel and responds in the thread, carrying the thread's conversation history as context. This allows multi-turn conversation without a database.

# Slack bolt @mention handler (pip install slack-bolt)
from slack_bolt import App
from slack_bolt.adapter.flask import SlackRequestHandler
import anthropic, os

slack_app = App(token=os.environ['SLACK_BOT_TOKEN'],
                signing_secret=os.environ['SLACK_SIGNING_SECRET'])
claude = anthropic.Anthropic()

SYSTEM_PROMPTS = {
    'C_SUPPORT_CHANNEL_ID': 'You are a Tier 1 support assistant. Check policy docs before answering.',
    'C_ENG_CHANNEL_ID':     'You are a code reviewer. Focus on correctness and security.',
    'default':              'You are a helpful assistant. Be concise.',
}

@slack_app.event('app_mention')
def handle_mention(event, say, client):
    channel_id = event['channel']
    thread_ts = event.get('thread_ts') or event['ts']

    # Fetch thread history for multi-turn context
    history = client.conversations_replies(
        channel=channel_id, ts=thread_ts, limit=20
    )
    messages = []
    bot_id = client.auth_test()['user_id']
    for msg in history['messages']:
        role = 'assistant' if msg.get('user') == bot_id else 'user'
        text = msg.get('text', '').replace(f'<@{bot_id}>', '').strip()
        if text:
            messages.append({'role': role, 'content': text})

    system = SYSTEM_PROMPTS.get(channel_id, SYSTEM_PROMPTS['default'])
    response = claude.messages.create(
        model='claude-sonnet-4-6',
        max_tokens=1000,
        system=system,
        messages=messages or [{'role': 'user', 'content': event['text']}]
    )
    say(text=response.content[0].text, thread_ts=thread_ts)

Key design choice: the `SYSTEM_PROMPTS` dict maps channel IDs to different instructions. The support channel gets a support-oriented prompt; the engineering channel gets a code-reviewer prompt. This gives different teams different Claude behaviour without separate bots.
Google Workspace Integration
Google Apps Script Add-on (Docs, Sheets, Slides)

Google Apps Script runs inside Google Workspace apps and can call external APIs including the Anthropic API. An Apps Script add-on adds a sidebar or menu item to Docs/Sheets/Slides that lets users interact with Claude without leaving the document.

The API key is stored in Script Properties (not in the code) to avoid accidental exposure. `UrlFetchApp.fetch` is the Apps Script HTTP client — it runs server-side, so there is no CORS issue and the API key never reaches the browser.
Google Drive via Cowork Connector

For interactive use (reading files in a conversation rather than processing them programmatically), the Google Drive OAuth connector in Cowork is the right approach. A member connects their Google account once; Claude can then search Drive, read files, and reference their content in any Shared Project conversation.
Use case: a support agent asks Claude 'what does our refund policy say about digital products?' — Claude searches Drive for the policy document and quotes the relevant section.
Use case: a manager asks Claude to summarise the last three quarterly reports stored in a Drive folder.
Limitation: the connector accesses files the member has permission to read. Shared Drives are included if the member has access.
Limitation: the Cowork connector is interactive only — it cannot be used in automated batch processing. Use the API for batch workflows.
Microsoft 365 Integration
Microsoft Teams Bot

Teams bots are built on the Microsoft Bot Framework and can call the Anthropic API. The architecture is similar to the Slack mention bot: a message event triggers the bot, the bot calls Claude, and the response appears in the channel or chat.

The key Teams-specific consideration is authentication. Teams bots authenticate via Azure App Registration. The bot's Anthropic API key should be stored in Azure Key Vault and retrieved at runtime — not in the Teams app manifest or environment variables on shared compute.
Word / Excel / PowerPoint Add-ins (Office Add-ins)

Office Add-ins run in a task pane inside Word, Excel, and PowerPoint. They are web applications (HTML/JavaScript) hosted on your infrastructure and loaded within the Office app. The task pane calls the Anthropic API via a backend server (not directly from the browser, to avoid exposing the API key).
App
High-value Claude use case
Word
Improve selected text, proofread, translate, summarise the document, expand bullet points into prose
Excel
Explain a formula, generate a formula from a plain-language description, summarise data in a selected range
PowerPoint
Generate speaker notes for selected slides, rewrite slide bullets to be more concise
CRM and ERP Integration Considerations

CRM systems (Salesforce, HubSpot) and ERP systems (SAP, Oracle) contain the most sensitive business data in most organisations. Integrating Claude with these systems requires extra care around data minimisation, write access, and human oversight.
Read-First, Write-Later Principle

Start with read-only integrations. A Claude integration that can read a Salesforce account's history and draft a follow-up email is valuable and relatively low-risk. A Claude integration that can update account records, create opportunities, or send emails automatically is high-risk and requires explicit human approval before any write action.
Safe pattern:
Claude reads CRM data → proposes a draft action → human reviews and executes the action manually.
Risky pattern:
Claude reads CRM data → executes the action automatically. Only appropriate for low-stakes, reversible actions (e.g. adding a tag) with full audit logging.
Never automate:
Email sending to external clients, financial record updates, contract creation, or any action that cannot be trivially reversed — without a human approval step.
Data Minimisation for CRM Integrations
CRM records often contain PII — customer names, contact details, financial data. When building an MCP server or API integration for a CRM, minimise the data Claude receives:
Return only the fields Claude needs for the specific task. If Claude is summarising a support case, it does not need the customer's full billing history.
Mask or redact PII where Claude does not need the actual value. If Claude is categorising cases by product, it does not need the customer's email address.
Use service accounts with minimal CRM permissions. The integration should only be able to read the records and fields its use case requires.
Testing and Monitoring Tool Integrations

Every production Claude integration in a tool like Slack or Google Workspace needs a lightweight test harness and ongoing monitoring. Unlike a static feature, LLM integrations can degrade silently when upstream APIs change, model behaviour shifts, or usage patterns hit rate limits.
Pre-launch Checklist
Functional test: run 10–20 representative prompts through the integration and verify outputs are correct, on-topic, and formatted correctly for the target tool (Slack markdown ≠ HTML ≠ plain text).
Rate limit test: send 50 requests in rapid succession and verify the integration handles 429 responses gracefully — retry with backoff, not silent failure.
Error handling: pull the API key, verify the integration returns a user-friendly error message rather than an uncaught exception or an empty response.

Cost check: estimate monthly cost at expected request volume. For Slack bots that respond to every @mention in a busy channel, the bill can be large. Set a spend alert in the Anthropic Console before launch.

Security review: confirm the API key is in a secrets manager, not hardcoded; confirm the bot cannot be prompted to reveal system prompts or credentials; confirm the integration validates that requests come from Slack/Google (signature verification).
Ongoing Monitoring
Error rate:
Alert if >1% of requests return an error. Common causes: API key expired, model deprecated, upstream rate limit hit, payload too large.
Response latency: track p95 response time. Latency increases can indicate upstream issues or large context sizes.
Cost per integration: break out API spend by integration using separate API keys. A single noisy integration should not obscure cost signals from others.

Output quality sampling: monthly, manually review 20 random outputs from each production integration. Qualitative review catches prompt drift, knowledge file staleness, and model behaviour changes that error metrics miss.

Model upgrade caution: when Anthropic releases a new model version, do not automatically upgrade production integrations. Test on a non-production instance first. New model versions can change output formatting, verbosity, or instruction-following behaviour in ways that break downstream parsing or violate the system prompt's style rules.
Summary

Integrating Claude into Slack, Google Workspace, Microsoft 365, GitHub, Jira, and CRM systems requires matching the integration approach to available engineering resources and the nature of the workflow. Cowork OAuth connectors handle interactive human-in-the-loop cases for supported tools without engineering. Slack and Teams bots require 4 hours to 2 weeks of engineering depending on the desired tier of sophistication. Google Apps Script and Office Add-ins bring Claude into document editors with moderate engineering effort. CRM and ERP integrations should start read-only, use data minimisation, and require human approval for any write or external action.
